verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: There are three stages of Jerome’s work of Scripture Translation.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: The first is during his stay at Rome, a.d.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: 382–385, when he translated only from the Greek—the New Testament from the Greek mss., and the Book of Psalms from the LXX.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: The second is the period immediately after his settlement at Bethlehem, when he translated still from the LXX., but marked with obeli and asterisks the passages in which that version differed from the Hebrew: the third from a.d.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: 390–404, in which he translated directly from the Hebrew.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: The work of the second period is that which is now before us.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: The whole of the Old Testament was translated from the LXX. (see his Apology, book ii. c.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: 24), but most of it was lost during his lifetime (see Letters CXXXIV. (end) and CXVI.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: What remains is the Book of Job, the Psalms, Chronicles, the Books of Solomon, and Tobit and Judith.
